data.body:
PASS 1 plan-review (T10 — Infrastructure / IaC overlay). Overlay APPLIES: Terraform-provisioned cloud infra (EC2, dedicated EBS data volume, EIP, IAM roles/policies, DLM, SNS/CloudWatch, S3 remote state, a GitHub ruleset) + an internet-facing Gerrit code-review server. Verdict: STRONG on state/locking, least-privilege IAM, idempotency/drift ownership, blast-radius prevent_destroy, cost/sizing, and observability/teardown — TWO grounded coaching findings, both independently re-verified against infra/ code: (1) the human/admin endpoint-access contract for Gerrit itself (b2), (2) encryption-at-rest on the stateful, token-bearing EBS volume (d/e).

AFFIRMATIONS (T10 dimensions this plan satisfies — code-corroborated):
- (a) STATE + plan-before-apply: S3 remote backend with native use_lockfile locking (no local state; TF >= 1.10 floor), one-time local-state bootstrap for the state bucket; `terraform plan`-before-`apply` reaffirmed epic-wide (esp. the high-stakes S6 ruleset replace) and in S7's runbook AC.
- (b) LEAST-PRIVILEGE IAM: SSM read scoped to `arn:...:parameter/rebar/prod/*` (not `*`), `kms:Decrypt` bounded by a `kms:ViaService` condition, single-owner role contract (S1 creates; S2/S4a only ADD separately-named scoped inline policies); replication deploy key single-repo write-only. No wildcard admin; no long-lived creds in IaC (SSM SecureString + `__BOT_TOKEN__` placeholder substituted at boot). IMDSv2 required (main.tf:50-54).
- (c) IDEMPOTENCY & DRIFT: single-owner contracts for the EC2 role, `gerrit.config` (S2 base + S3 `[change]`-only read-modify-write), and the DLM policy (S1 owns; S7 only ASSERTS); IaC-rendered configs under infra/, not hand-edited; `ignore_changes=[ami]` avoids force-replace churn on newly-published AMIs.
- (d) BLAST RADIUS: `prevent_destroy` on the NoteDb data volume (main.tf:27-40), EIP, and state bucket; data volume deliberately SEPARATE from root; freeze-and-restore posture + "merged code always safe on the GitHub mirror" bound RPO; single-env PoC explicitly accepted.
- (f) COST & SIZING: single Graviton t4g.large (~8 GB), pinned ~2 GB heap, gp3, default VPC, no ALB/NAT, SSM Parameter Store (free) over Secrets Manager, DLM retain=7.
- (g) OBSERVABILITY & TEARDOWN: Rebar/Gate:GerritReachable metric + alarm (<1 for 2×5min, treat_missing_data=breaching), EC2 status-check backstops, SNS→SSM endpoint; reproducible as-code with a non-destructive restore drill; documented state-bucket teardown.

FINDINGS (2):

- [ ] Specify how HUMAN principals (developers AND admins) authenticate to the internet-facing Gerrit web UI/REST (:443) and Gerrit SSH (:29418) — a named identity mechanism (OAuth/OIDC/SSO/LDAP/HTTP-auth) OR an explicit, justified no-auth rationale that is safe for an internet-exposed box. As written, the plan opens 443 + 29418 to `0.0.0.0/0` (infra/terraform/network.tf:28-55) behind a public EIP + DNS (rebar.solutions.navateam.com), but the ONLY configured Gerrit auth mode is `auth.type = DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT` (infra/compose/gerrit.config:75), under which any anonymous internet visitor can "become ANY account" — including Administrators (the first dev-login account is auto-added to Administrators per bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh). That is not authentication; it is anonymous admin assumption from the open internet, which ALSO defeats the plan's core integrity claim (the `LLM-Review` gate/submit-req can be self-voted/bypassed by anyone who "becomes" a Service User or admin). The config itself flags this as unresolved ("PoC ONLY ... S3/S4a MAY refine to a real auth type ... do NOT leave this on a box that holds anything sensitive"). Machine credentials the plan specifies (deploy key, bot HTTP token, SSM secrets) authenticate MACHINES, not human/admin access — per T10 they do not satisfy this sub-check. This is a deny-by-default under-specified (here, affirmatively unsafe) human-auth contract on an internet-reachable service.
  - criteria: [T10]
  - location: AC1 ("reverse proxy on 443; Gerrit HTTP 8080 + SSH 29418"); infra/terraform/network.tf:28-55; infra/compose/gerrit.config:69-75; infra/scripts/bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh
  - evidence: code fact — `auth.type = DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT` is the ONLY configured auth mode (gerrit.config:75); network.tf:33,54 open 443 + 29418 to `0.0.0.0/0` with a public EIP/DNS; config phrase "S3/S4a MAY refine to a real auth type ... do NOT leave this on a box that holds anything sensitive" = explicitly deferred/unresolved; ABSENCE of any named human-auth mechanism (OAuth/OIDC/SSO/LDAP/HTTP-auth) in any AC/ADR/plan section; T10 last checklist item (human + admin auth on every stood-up network-reachable service; service-to-service creds do NOT count).
  - suggested_fix: Add an AC + ADR to S2 (or S3/S4a) that pins Gerrit's human-auth posture BEFORE the box is internet-exposed: EITHER (a) set `auth.type` to a real IdP (`OAUTH` via gerrit-oauth-provider with GitHub/Google OIDC, or `HTTP` behind an authenticating nginx layer) with anonymous write disabled; OR (b) if a real IdP is genuinely out of PoC scope, restrict the 443 + 29418 ingress `cidr_blocks` to a trusted operator CIDR / VPN / SG allowlist (not `0.0.0.0/0`) and record that network-perimeter restriction as the explicit, justified no-auth rationale — so `DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT` is never reachable from the open internet. State it in the plan rather than deferring with "S3/S4a may refine".

- [ ] Specify `encrypted = true` (+ a KMS/CMK) for the dedicated `/var/gerrit` NoteDb EBS data volume AND the instance root device, so they and their inherited DLM snapshots are encrypted at rest. This is a T10(d)/(e) stateful-resource + secrets concern: the data volume is the plan's designated `prevent_destroy` stateful resource AND it stores NoteDb's `refs/meta/config`, which per ADR-0014 holds the live webhook bot token — so an unencrypted stateful volume is a plaintext-secret-at-rest gap, and DLM snapshots INHERIT the source volume's (currently absent) encryption. The plan's "NoteDb persistence + backup" bullet / AC1 name only "a dedicated gp3 EBS volume ... with prevent_destroy + DLM daily snapshots" and never state encryption; code confirms the gap — `aws_ebs_volume.data` (main.tf:27-40) sets `type = "gp3"` + `prevent_destroy` but declares NO `encrypted`, `root_block_device` (main.tf:56-59) likewise, and a repo-wide `search infra/ "encrypted"` returns ZERO matches (so even the root device is unencrypted, contra derisk note E1's "gp3-encrypted" claim, which dry-ran only the root-device shape).
  - criteria: [T10]
  - location: Decomposition & resolved design questions → "NoteDb persistence + backup" bullet; AC1; infra/terraform/main.tf:27-59, infra/terraform/backup.tf
  - evidence: plan phrase "NoteDb on a dedicated gp3 EBS volume at `/var/gerrit` with `prevent_destroy` + DLM daily snapshots (S1)"; code fact — `aws_ebs_volume.data` (main.tf:27-40) and `root_block_device` (main.tf:56-59) declare no `encrypted`, and `search infra/ "encrypted"` → 0 hits; ADR-0014: the bot token lives in `refs/meta/config`, i.e. on this volume; T10(e) no plaintext secrets at rest; T10(d) stateful-resource safety.
  - suggested_fix: Add `encrypted = true` (AWS-managed EBS key or a project CMK) to `aws_ebs_volume.data` and `root_block_device`; add a line to S1's volume AC stating that encryption propagates to the DLM snapshots so the token-bearing `refs/meta/config` is encrypted at rest.

No other NEW T10 defects — state/locking, least-privilege IAM, idempotency/drift ownership, blast-radius prevent_destroy, cost/sizing, and observability/teardown are all specified and code-corroborated. The two gaps are the unspecified (and currently unsafe) human/admin authentication to the internet-facing Gerrit service and the missing at-rest encryption on the token-bearing stateful volume.
